commands.repair_reason: The blocker is security-sensitive maintainer/contributor follow-up: fix the disableTools native-surface gap and provide redacted live Telegram/EVE proof before merge, not a ClawSweeper repair job.; Needs attention: The diff narrows a security-sensitive policy bypass, but the new native tool gate still appears to leave disableTools Codex app-server runs exposed.; Review finding: [P1] Honor disableTools in native tool... (sha=643fd4bfe79f852cbde47517cdb36725e0ce8332)
commands.review_summary:
The PR collapses deny-all plugin-harness policies into empty allowlists and disables Codex app-server dynamic, native code-mode, user MCP, and app-default tool surfaces for restricted runs, with focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main treats empty `toolsAllow` as unrestricted dynamic tools and always enables Codex native code mode. I did not run a live Telegram/Codex reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The blocker is security-sensitive maintainer/contributor follow-up: fix the `disableTools` native-surface gap and provide redacted live Telegram/EVE proof before merge, not a ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: Fix denied Codex app-server tool policy
target.body:
## Summary
- Honor deny-all (`deny: ["*"]`) and restricted runtime tool policies in the Codex app-server path by disabling native code mode, built-in environments, user MCP projection, dynamic tools, and Codex app defaults for restricted runs.
- Collapse plugin-harness deny-all policy into `toolsAllow: []` at runtime, matching PI behavior while preserving the config/source-of-truth shape as `deny: ["*"]`.
- Add sender/chat-specific refusal wording for denied users and keep restricted Codex turns transient so an allowed sender's existing tool-capable thread binding is preserved.

## Root Cause
Channel sender/chat deny-all policy is configured as `deny: ["*"]` and was reproduced through Telegram. Plugin harnesses did not consistently collapse that deny-all policy into an empty runtime allowlist before invoking Codex. In the Codex app-server path, an explicit empty allowlist still left native app-server surfaces available: code mode could remain enabled, built-in environments could still register shell/file tools, user MCP servers could still project, and Codex app defaults were not always explicitly disabled. That let a denied sender appear to have `toolCount: 0` while native Codex tool surfaces could still run.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: A sender/chat/runtime `deny: ["*"]` policy now produces a restricted Codex run that has no OpenClaw dynamic tools, no Codex native code-mode shell/file environment, no user MCP projection, and explicit Codex app default denial.

Real environment tested: Local temp worktree from current `origin/main`, plus the live gateway container rebuilt from this branch. The live proof below verifies the patched code is installed and healthy in the deployed OpenClaw gateway; the unit/runtime tests verify the exact restricted-turn request payload.

Before proof: In the pre-patch live gateway behavior, a denied-sender request under `deny: ["*"]` could still result in Codex-native shell/file tool calls and a completed edit even though policy intended a no-tool run. The pre-patch code also treated `toolsAllow: []` as an unrestricted dynamic-tool catalog, kept Codex code mode enabled, did not send `environments: []`, and did not always send an explicit `apps._default` denial.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `docker ps --filter name=jackie-openclaw-gateway --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Status}}' && docker exec jackie-openclaw-gateway sh -lc "grep -R -n -E 'this sender is not allowed by policy|nativeToolSurfaceRestricted|apps._default denial patch|environments: \[\]' /app/dist /app/extensions 2>/dev/null | head -20"`

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the rebuilt gateway container:

```text
NAMES                     IMAGE                STATUS
jackie-openclaw-gateway   openclaw-eve:local   Up 23 minutes (healthy)
/app/dist/selection-CRZITzTC.js:15231:const PLUGIN_HARNESS_SENDER_DENY_ALL_PROMPT = "Tool and file actions are disabled for this sender by chat policy. If asked to edit files or use tools, say this sender is not allowed by policy; do not imply retrying will help.";
/app/dist/thread-lifecycle-BnrqCSE8.js:1300:		...options.nativeCodeModeEnabled === false ? { environments: [] } : {},
/app/extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts:583:    ...(options.nativeCodeModeEnabled === false ? { environments: [] } : {}),
/app/extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts:1137:    const nativeToolSurfaceRestricted = !nativeToolSurfaceEnabled;
/app/extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts:1139:      nativeToolSurfaceRestricted || shouldBuildCodexPluginThreadConfig(pluginConfig);
/app/extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts:1141:    // carries the explicit apps._default denial patch without app/list.
```

Observed result after fix: The live gateway is running the patched build with the sender-policy refusal prompt, native-tool restriction branch, built-in environment denial, and Codex app-default denial present. Focused regression tests also verify sender-specific `deny: ["*"]` becomes `toolsAllow: []`, PI harness params remain unchanged, restricted Codex `thread/start` receives `dynamicTools: []`, `environments: []`, `features.code_mode: false`, `features.code_mode_only: false`, `apps._default` disabled, no `app/list`, no user MCP servers, and transient restricted turns do not overwrite an existing plugin-enabled thread binding.

What was not tested: A post-patch live Telegram roundtrip through the deployed gateway was not completed. Broad `pnpm check` / full CI was not run locally.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts`
- `~/.codex/skills/codex-review/scripts/codex-review --mode local` (`codex-review clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`)
- Live gateway container rebuilt/recreated from this branch and verified healthy with patched code present.

## What was not tested
- Post-patch live Telegram roundtrip through the deployed gateway.
- Broad `pnpm check` / full CI locally.
